synopsis.generic: As each day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato present the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy, Nate Byrne and Lillian Rangiah.
synopsis.episode: Start the day with the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

synopsis.generic: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.
synopsis.episode: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

synopsis.generic: Married police detectives Vanessa and Cole are finally leaving their cop careers in the Australian police force behind and have just invested in their dream - a cute 13 room motel in a sunny, oceanside New Zealand town.
synopsis.episode: Married ex-cops Vinny and Cole have swapped the homicide beat in Sydney for their retirement dream, running a retro seaside motel in a coastal New Zealand town. But it seems murder and mayhem have a new place to stay.

synopsis.generic: Lauren and Ned abandon their exciting city lives and return to the tropical north of Australia when they learn that they are now the guardians of Lauren's orphaned eight-year-old niece.
synopsis.episode: Lauren and Ned's perfect Adelaide life is interrupted when tragedy strikes Lauren's family and they're forced to return to Darwin, faced with a brand new curveball: a cheeky, grieving young girl named Taya.

synopsis.generic: Take a road less travelled and visit some of the small towns and communities that make Australia special. Heather Ewart and guest presenters return to discover more remarkable stories and inspiring people you'll never forget.
synopsis.episode: Champion Paralympian Kurt Fearnley finds a heart of gold shining brightly amongst the townsfolk of Tennant Creek. Locals are overcoming intergenerational trauma by nourishing residents with great education, art and religion.

synopsis.generic: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.
synopsis.episode: The leading news at lunchtime. Kathryn Robinson brings you today's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

synopsis.generic: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.
synopsis.episode: The interstate invader costing millions in South Australia's Riverland region; The environmental impact of buffel grass; and the diagnostic tool reducing dairy product wastage.

description.episode-title: Aaron Wyatt, Courtney Act, Vanessa Amorosi & Hamish Blake

synopsis.generic: Join Adam Hills, Myf Warhurst and Alan Brough for a brand new series of your favourite music quiz show. There'll be new games, fantastic live music and, as always, you can play along with all your old favourites.
synopsis.episode: Putting their hands in the air like they really care this week are Hamish Blake, Vanessa Amorosi, Courtney Act and Aaron Wyatt. Special guest Michala Banas joins Alan as fill-in captain. Can you work out the secret song?

synopsis.generic: Anh Do paints some of Australia's most famous faces and captures their personalities on the canvas. The charismatic comedian, writer and Archibald Finalist delves into the inspirational stories that have shaped their lives.
synopsis.episode: Anh is joined by Australian screen legend Sigrid Thornton for an intimate chat about the people and moments that have shaped her life, worldview and career. Will Anh be able to capture her range and her warmth? (Final)

synopsis.generic: Art detectives Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue and deception.
synopsis.episode: The team seek to find out the truth about three mystery portraits, one of which might be an early work by modern artist Willem de Kooning. The hunt for evidence takes them to Berlin, Miami and Belgium. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: When two respected officers of the court are murdered, Brackenreid fears he's next on the hit list; meanwhile, Murdoch interviews for the Inspector's position at a new station.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: A woman searching for the father who promised that he would come back for her. And in Somerset, a woman searching for her twin sisters who vanished from her life when they were babies.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team roll out their 40th anniversary tour as they scour the country in search of hidden antique treasures.
synopsis.episode: Fiona and the team head to Leicester and for the first time in 40 years of Antiques Roadshow, the location is a Victorian sewage pumping station - now Leicester City Council's Museum of Technology.

synopsis.generic: The hardest man in television, Tom Gleeson sets out to find Australia's hardest quiz champion, pitting four contestants against each other in a battle of attrition.
synopsis.episode: Tom Gleeson's Big Brass Mug is up for grabs again! Alan, Liz, Michael and Kim go head-to-head-to-head-to-head on their expert topics: Meryl Streep, the AFLW, the Khmer Empire and My Little Pony.

synopsis.generic: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Guy Stayner (Mon-Fri).
synopsis.episode: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Tasmanians. Presented by Guy Stayner.

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

In 2026, Logie-award-winning documentary program Australian Story celebrates 30 years sharing compelling stories about real people that illuminate, entertain and inspire. The power of the personal. Presented by Leigh Sales.
synopsis.episode: Who cares for our children when we're gone if they can't care for themselves? As Deirdre Croft faces the end of her life, she's on a mission to ensure her son is looked after when she's gone.

synopsis.generic: David Speers moderates this new discussion program bringing together ordinary citizens, community leaders, experts and policymakers to discuss topical issues of national significance.
synopsis.episode: The first ABC National Forum will focus on the lives and experiences of Jewish Australians, including issues arising from the Bondi terrorist attack. David Speers facilitates a wide-ranging conversation among the audience.

synopsis.generic: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

As Trump steamrolls through the second year of his second term, Chas and John are here to guide you through the mayhem: covering Iran, ICE, tariffs, NATO, Russia/Ukraine, Gaza, affordability, Greenland, the midterms and more.
synopsis.episode: Chas and John cover the latest on the Middle East, Trump fires Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem, plus washup from the Texas primaries as James Talarico won the Democratic nomination and Republicans go to a runoff.

synopsis.generic: We are your passport to the world. Our stories, told through the ABC's network of foreign correspondents, will take you to incredible places where you'll meet the people and see the forces shaping our world.
synopsis.episode: Switzerland's iconic mountains are truly one of the wonders of the world, famous for their dramatic snow-capped peaks. But the Swiss Alps are under threat, in turn putting centuries old villages below them in grave danger.

synopsis.generic: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Michael Tetlow.

Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.
Charlie Pickering is ready for 2026 as The Weekly returns with TV's best wrap of the week's news. We watch it all, so you don't have to.
synopsis.episode: Charlie tidies up Trump's Middle East mess, fixes Albo's one-word difficulty and hits the dump button on Kyle and Jackie-O while Rhys explains why everything isn't for everyone and why that's perfectly ok.

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: When two respected officers of the court are murdered, Brackenreid fears he's next on the hit list; meanwhile, Murdoch interviews for the Inspector's position at a new station.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: A woman searching for the father who promised that he would come back for her. And in Somerset, a woman searching for her twin sisters who vanished from her life when they were babies.

description.censor-warning: Adult Themes, Coarse Language, Drug Use, Horror, Nudity, Sex Scenes, Sexual References, Violence

synopsis.generic: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. We curate the best clips of all time to surprise and entertain you for hours.
Australia's Most Popular Lifestyle series returns for another season of gardening know-how and inspiration. So, let's get stuck in with Logie winning host Costa Georgiadis!
synopsis.episode: Clarence Slockee visits an herbalist's home garden, Costa Georgiadis drops by the home of former GA presenter Mary Moody, Jane Edmanson discovers the art of hedging and Tino Carnevale visits a garden designed for wildlife.

description.series-title: A Bite To Eat With Alice
description.epg-title1: A Bite To Eat With Alice

synopsis.generic: A Bite to Eat with Alice is a cooking show dedicated to creating delicious food. No matter your skill level, host Alice and her guest - who may not be able to cook toast - will inspire you to experiment in the kitchen.
synopsis.episode: Star of Love on the Spectrum and Austin, Michael Theo looks to Alice for tips on cooking for that special someone.

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

As each day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato present the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy, Nate Byrne and Lillian Rangiah.

synopsis.episode: Who cares for our children when we're gone if they can't care for themselves? As Deirdre Croft faces the end of her life, she's on a mission to ensure her son is looked after when she's gone.

synopsis.generic: As Trump steamrolls through the second year of his second term, Chas and John are here to guide you through the mayhem: covering Iran, ICE, tariffs, NATO, Russia/Ukraine, Gaza, affordability, Greenland, the midterms and more.
synopsis.episode: Chas and John cover the latest on the Middle East, Trump fires Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem, plus washup from the Texas primaries as James Talarico won the Democratic nomination and Republicans go to a runoff.

synopsis.generic: Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: At undisclosed locations around the world, 'preppers' are skilling up and hunkering down. They're getting ready for the end of days with bunkers, stockpiled weapons, food stores and survival training.

synopsis.generic: 'Unprecedented' is a word we hear a lot lately. But the topics in the news are rarely unprecedented, often we've seen it all before. What can we learn from history? Matt Bevan digs into the past to understand current events.
synopsis.episode: In the 1950s three quarters of Australians were homeowners. Nowadays, house prices have sky rocketed and younger generations largely feel locked out. So where did it all go wrong?

synopsis.generic: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.
Join DI Neville Parker and his team as they solve intriguing crimes on the idyllic Saint Marie. With new and returning faces and a surprising enemy, this season is filled with twists and turns.
synopsis.episode: We take a trip down memory lane as commissioner Selwyn Patterson celebrates 50 years of police service at the Yacht Club, but the day takes a turn when he is shot and rushed to hospital.

synopsis.generic: Coverage of Question Time from Australia's Federal Parliament in Canberra.
synopsis.episode: Federal Parliament returns for a new sitting week. Coverage of Question Time from the House of Representatives.

synopsis.generic: Art detectives Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue, and deception.
synopsis.episode: Art detectives Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery. In this episode, they take on one of the most important cases they've ever faced.

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: Fannie Robinson stands out as the most viable suspect in the death of her husband, boxer Amos Robinson, but Murdoch's strong intuition tells him otherwise.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two lives overshadowed by the past: a man searching for the brother given up after a wartime affair, and a woman longing to understand the reasons behind her adoption. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team roll out their 40th anniversary tour as they scour the country in search of hidden antique treasures.
synopsis.episode: Fiona and the team are in Northern Ireland's Belfast. They settle into a prime location at the magnificent Parliament Buildings in Stormont Estate, which has played a huge role in Northern Ireland's political history.

synopsis.generic: The hardest man in television, Tom Gleeson sets out to find Australia's hardest quiz champion, pitting four contestants against each other in a battle of attrition.
synopsis.episode: Elvis superfan David goes up against Susan with the topic: Treehouse book series, Spencer with the subject: Vespas and Shah with the topic: Rugby World Cup. No matter who wins, Tom Gleeson is still the king.

synopsis.generic: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Guy Stayner (Mon-Fri).
synopsis.episode: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Tasmanians. Presented by Selina Bryan.

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

We are your passport to the world. Our stories, told through the ABC's network of foreign correspondents, will take you to incredible places where you'll meet the people and see the forces shaping our world.
synopsis.episode: A revolution is taking place in Indonesia. President Prabowo Subianto is nationalising and militarising his nation's economy to deliver a self-reliant future. But some are warning Indonesia could be returning to the Suharto era.

synopsis.generic: Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: We meet devout members of the Aetherius Society, an international religion that believes life on Earth is guided by advanced extra-terrestrial civilisations existing in other realms on planets in our solar system.

synopsis.generic: 'Unprecedented' is a word we hear a lot lately. But the topics in the news are rarely unprecedented, often we've seen it all before. What can we learn from history? Matt Bevan digs into the past to understand current events.
synopsis.episode: In 1959 travel was getting faster and many expected we'd be routinely zipping to other planets in no time. But now, our transport is not much faster than it was back then, so why are we still so slow? (Part 3 of 4)

synopsis.generic: The six-part arts series with Virginia Trioli is back for a third season, exploring the essence of imagination and creativity with some of Australia's best creative minds.
synopsis.episode: Jimmy Barnes jumps into the car with Virginia for a ride and a chat and to turn up the cassette player on the music that formed and informed him and that continues to drive him into a mature life of music making.

synopsis.generic: Compass explores the Australian soul - our beliefs, our ethical dilemmas and the changing face of our spirituality, with host, popular journalist and broadcaster, Indira Naidoo.
synopsis.episode: Four friends went to a Jewish celebration at Bondi Beach, but only three returned. Compass joins friends and family to honour their beloved Marika Pogany and explore Bondi's Jewish history. Introduced by Mia Freedman.

synopsis.generic: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Michael Tetlow.

Australia's Most Popular Lifestyle series returns for another season of gardening know-how and inspiration. So, let's get stuck in with Logie winning host Costa Georgiadis!

synopsis.generic: Take a road less travelled to the small towns and communities that make Australia special. Join Lisa Millar and guest presenters as they discover remarkable stories and inspiring people you'll never forget.
synopsis.episode: In the first episode of the season, Lisa Millar takes "Australia's longest driveway" all the way to Shark Bay, in Western Australia's remote Gascoyne region.

description.episode-title: Allegra Spender - Independent MP for Wentworth

synopsis.generic: The National Press Club hosts public debates and discussions that shape Australia now and into the future. Home to national conversations with decision-makers and influencers; driving the national debate forward.
synopsis.episode: Allegra Spender, the Independent MP for Wentworth, addresses the National Press Club on the topic "Tax White Paper #1: Personal Taxation Fit for the Future".

synopsis.generic: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.
Coverage of Question Time from Australia's Federal Parliament in Canberra.
synopsis.episode: The parliamentary sitting week continues with coverage of Question Time from the House of Representatives.

synopsis.generic: Art detectives Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue, and deception.
synopsis.episode: Fiona Bruce and the team come down under for their first Australian investigation. Can they prove an online purchase from an English auction site is a lost work by Tom Roberts, considered one of Australia's greatest artists.

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: Sceptical Murdoch teams with his hero, Arthur Conan Doyle, to solve a murder that was revealed during a seance.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two of our youngest ever searchers, looking for their birth family. A man whose last contact with his father was when he was six and a woman whose life was transported from Bogota when she was given up for adoption.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team roll out their 40th anniversary tour as they scour the country in search of hidden antique treasures.
synopsis.episode: Fiona and the team return to the Abbey Sewage Pumping station in Leicester - now the city's Museum of Technology. Complete with four working beam engines, it's a fine example of Victorian engineering.

synopsis.generic: Gold Logie-winning comedian Tom Gleeson has managed to herd a new bunch of self-described experts into the hallowed ABC studios to undergo the HARDEST challenge in TV, or probably the whole world.
synopsis.episode: Tom Gleeson hosts a clash of epic proportions on Hard Quiz! Where else would you see Godzilla go head-to-head with Marilyn Monroe, Swan Lake, and the whale shark? Nowhere, that's where.

description.episode-title: Everything's Better Down Where It's Wetter

synopsis.generic: Filmed on location in a real museum after dark, surrounded by priceless artefacts, taxidermy and historical curiosities, Alex Lee guides four celebrity players through a series of challenges and quizzes.
synopsis.episode: After hours at the museum, Alex Lee welcomes Luke McGregor, Geraldine Hickey, Sashi Perera and Bjorn Stewart all chasing the ultimate honour: exhibition glory.

synopsis.generic: Charlie Pickering is ready for 2026 as The Weekly returns with TV's best wrap of the week's news. We watch it all, so you don't have to.
synopsis.episode: If you still want to know what's happening without having to follow the daily news, Charlie Pickering watches the week's events to bring you the best wrap-up and a few laughs. With Abbey Gelmi and Hannah Gadsby.

synopsis.generic: In the grip of a mid-life crisis, Roland meets the eternal optimist Samantha and her life-loving group of dog-parkers. As Roland begrudgingly keeps returning to the park, he realises he might need a community to belong to.
synopsis.episode: It's the day of Samantha and Steve's wedding. Roland is thrilled to have Mia home again but distracted by his growing feelings for Samantha. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Long running comedy quiz hosted by Sandi Toksvig which asks impossible questions and doesn't care whether the answers are "right or not" - just as long as they are interesting.
synopsis.episode: Sandi Toksvig goes a-wooing with Rosie Jones, Larry Dean, Maisie Adam and Alan Davies.

Asking the most outrageous, uncomfortable and shocking questions sourced from the public to uncover the truth behind eight new groups of marginalised or misunderstood Australians.
synopsis.episode: Despite over 200 years of migration history, many Chinese-Australians continue to feel othered, still having to prove that they belong. We meet 8 Chinese-Australians, all with very different stories.

synopsis.episode: As the day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, interviews and the day's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather.

synopsis.generic: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.
synopsis.episode: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

description.episode-title: Everything's Better Down Where It's Wetter

synopsis.generic: Filmed on location in a real museum after dark, surrounded by priceless artefacts, taxidermy and historical curiosities, Alex Lee guides four celebrity players through a series of challenges and quizzes.
synopsis.episode: After hours at the museum, Alex Lee welcomes Luke McGregor, Geraldine Hickey, Sashi Perera and Bjorn Stewart all chasing the ultimate honour: exhibition glory.

synopsis.generic: Charlie Pickering is ready for 2026 as The Weekly returns with TV's best wrap of the week's news. We watch it all, so you don't have to.
synopsis.episode: If you still want to know what's happening without having to follow the daily news, Charlie Pickering watches the week's events to bring you the best wrap-up and a few laughs. With Abbey Gelmi and Hannah Gadsby.

synopsis.generic: Virginia Trioli is back in 2025 with a new season, travelling the country and being invited into the very heart of the creative process of some of our most distinctive and prolific artists and performers.
synopsis.episode: George Miller is a genuine Australian visionary and is recognised as one of the greatest action film directors of all time. He's a philosopher, an innovator and a storyteller - and he's not done with Mad Max yet.

synopsis.generic: Long running comedy quiz hosted by Sandi Toksvig which asks impossible questions and doesn't care whether the answers are "right or not" - just as long as they are interesting.
synopsis.episode: Sandi Toksvig goes a-wooing with Rosie Jones, Larry Dean, Maisie Adam and Alan Davies.

synopsis.generic: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.
The iconic Jersey-set detective series returns. When a member of one of Jersey's wealthiest families is murdered the police want their best detective on the case.
synopsis.episode: The series reaches its thrilling climax as Bergerac must save his daughter, confront his past and finally crack the case. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Coverage of Question Time from Australia's Federal Parliament in Canberra.
synopsis.episode: The parliamentary sitting week concludes with coverage of Question Time from the Senate. Parliament returns on Monday 23 March.

synopsis.generic: Art detectives Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue, and deception.
synopsis.episode: Philip Mould, Fiona Bruce and the team are on the trail of two pictures brought to their attention by viewers, both believed to be by Paul Gauguin - one of the giants of 19th-century art. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Sister Boniface - nun, moped rider, wine maker and part-time forensic scientist. If there's evidence to be found, Boniface will find it, with a little help from dashing DI Sam Gillespie.
synopsis.episode: Sparks fly as Sam and Ruth go undercover at a spiritual retreat for couples only.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two stories of new beginnings: a woman separated from her mother because of a tragic twist of fate; and our first transgender searcher, desperate to know if their birth mother will accept them.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team roll out their 40th anniversary tour as they scour the country in search of hidden antique treasures.
synopsis.episode: At Newcastle's Civic Centre, treasures include a tribute to Victorian heroine Grace Darling, who risked her life to save survivors of a wrecked steamship.

synopsis.generic: Gold Logie-winning comedian Tom Gleeson has managed to herd a new bunch of self-described experts into the hallowed ABC studios to undergo the HARDEST challenge in TV, or probably the whole world.
synopsis.episode: Four contestants vie for the Hard Quiz Big Brass Mug! Which topic will triumph: Rod Stewart, The Sopranos, or Bunnings... or will cholera wipe them out? Play along at home!

synopsis.generic: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Guy Stayner (Mon-Fri).
synopsis.episode: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Tasmanians. Presented by Guy Stayner.

synopsis.generic: Take a road less travelled to the small towns and communities that make Australia special. Join Lisa Millar and guest presenters as they discover remarkable stories and inspiring people you'll never forget.
synopsis.episode: Lisa Millar continues her exploration of Shark Bay, a World Heritage listed wilderness on the westernmost point of Western Australia. It's a vivid contrast of turquoise waters and red desert cliffs.

synopsis.generic: The six-part arts series with Virginia Trioli is back for a third season, exploring the essence of imagination and creativity with some of Australia's best creative minds.
synopsis.episode: The actor's actor, Hugo Weaving, finds the humanity within the fractured and complex men he plays in movies and films, but is still a beloved icon of the Australian stage.

synopsis.generic: The new season of this beloved series will pair up Muster Dogs alumni with some cleanskins who have never worked with dogs before to find out if you can train a farmer to train a Champion Muster Dog in just 12 months.
synopsis.episode: After a year-long experiment and a crash course in training a Muster Dog, our six rookies face their final challenge, where one dog will be crowned our fourth Champion Muster Dog. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: The complexities of unregulated sperm donation sees half siblings all around the world. We meet a father who has children in the hundreds. Many of the mothers can't afford IVF and instead choose to go down the private path.

synopsis.generic: Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: At undisclosed locations around the world, 'preppers' are skilling up and hunkering down. They're getting ready for the end of days with bunkers, stockpiled weapons, food stores and survival training.

synopsis.generic: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Michael Tetlow.

Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.
synopsis.episode: Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, investors, employees and businesses big and small. Presented by Alicia Barry.

synopsis.episode: Two stories of new beginnings: a woman separated from her mother because of a tragic twist of fate; and our first transgender searcher, desperate to know if their birth mother will accept them.

synopsis.generic: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. We curate the best clips of all time to surprise and entertain you for hours.
Australia's Most Popular Lifestyle series returns for another season of gardening know-how and inspiration. So, let's get stuck in with Logie winning host Costa Georgiadis!
synopsis.episode: Costa Georgiadis meets an inspiring gardener, Tino Carnevale sows spring crops indoors, Josh Byrne visits a landscape designer, Jane Edmanson visits a stunning garden in rural Victoria and Millie Ross builds a garden bench.

description.series-title: A Bite To Eat With Alice
description.epg-title1: A Bite To Eat With Alice

synopsis.generic: A Bite to Eat with Alice is a cooking show dedicated to creating delicious food. No matter your skill level, host Alice and her guest - who may not be able to cook toast - will inspire you to experiment in the kitchen.
synopsis.episode: TV presenting royalty Osher Gunsberg joins Alice as she makes him some delicious dishes to suit his dietaries, including an easy-to-make cheesecake, and they come up with a way to tear up the dancefloor at weddings! (Final)

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

As each day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato present the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy, Nate Byrne and Lillian Rangiah.
synopsis.episode: We're packing our bags and hitting the road in 2026 for a series of outside broadcasts. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato are joined by Catherine Murphy and Nate Byrne on the Gold Coast with the latest news, sport and weather.

synopsis.generic: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.
synopsis.episode: Stay in the know with what's going on this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Kathryn Robinson.

synopsis.generic: Compass explores the Australian soul - our beliefs, our ethical dilemmas and the changing face of our spirituality, with host, popular journalist and broadcaster, Indira Naidoo.
synopsis.episode: Four friends went to a Jewish celebration at Bondi Beach, but only three returned. Compass joins friends and family to honour their beloved Marika Pogany and explore Bondi's Jewish history. Introduced by Mia Freedman.

synopsis.generic: Follow Marc Fennell on a globe-trotting, emotional quest for the truth as he unravels the twisted mysteries behind six iconic and priceless objects taken by the British Empire and meets those who want them back.
synopsis.episode: Marc Fennell unravels the true history of the Koh I Noor diamond which sits at the heart of the Crown Jewels. But beyond the glitz, he reveals the tragedy of a 10-year-old Sikh boy ripped from his mother and kingdom.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team visit more of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, inviting the public to bring their antiques for examination and share stories of how they came to own them.
synopsis.episode: The team return to Wells in Somerset to see some Faberge jewellery and a plate by Pugin.

synopsis.generic: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.

The new season of this beloved series will pair up Muster Dogs alumni with some cleanskins who have never worked with dogs before to find out if you can train a farmer to train a Champion Muster Dog in just 12 months.
synopsis.episode: After a year-long experiment and a crash course in training a Muster Dog, our six rookies face their final challenge, where one dog will be crowned our fourth Champion Muster Dog. (Final)

synopsis.generic: When Charlie's best friend dies, leaving him to support his mate's grieving family, he is confronted with facing his own demons as well as the ghost of his dead friend haunting him.
synopsis.episode: As Charlie uncovers the mystery of the missing $30k, it only raises more questions as to why Nate chose to end his life. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue and deception.
synopsis.episode: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate more cases of art world mystery, intrigue and deception. Is a still life painting by William Nicholson bought in 2006 for £165,000 real or an elaborate fake?

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: When it is revealed that a man murdered on his wedding day was homosexual, Murdoch finds himself fighting his own beliefs.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two stories shaped by moments from British history: a family torn apart by the birth of a war time baby and a daughter given up against a backdrop of racism.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team roll out their 40th anniversary tour as they scour the country in search of hidden antique treasures.
synopsis.episode: Antiques Roadshow returns to Osborne on the Isle of Wight for a second helping of finds, including a Wedgwood vase and some food tins that tell a story of Colditz. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Gold Logie-winning comedian Tom Gleeson has managed to herd a new bunch of self-described experts into the hallowed ABC studios to undergo the HARDEST challenge in TV, or probably the whole world.
synopsis.episode: Fighting for Tom Gleeson's Big Brass Mug in this episode are Paula Abdul fan Matthew, 30 Rock fan Patrice, chocolate connoisseur Julie, and Sir Joh Bjelke-Petersen connoisseur Regan!

synopsis.generic: Inspiring, entertaining and full of practical advice, join Costa Georgiadis and the team as they unearth gardening ideas, meet avid gardeners and look at some of the most inspiring gardens from across the country.
synopsis.episode: Costa visits pop legend Leo Sayer in his rose-filled country garden, Sophie reveals her new outback patch, Guest Presenter Thanh the Fruit Nerd joins a feijoa fanatic, and we meet an ecologist growing tiny urban forests.

synopsis.generic: The multi award-winning much-loved crime drama Professor T is back for a third season. The Professor is in jail and must find a way to get his old life and job back.
synopsis.episode: Following a murder at a Criminology Conference, the Professor must prove his innocence to regain his reputation.

synopsis.episode: After hours at the museum, Alex Lee welcomes Luke McGregor, Geraldine Hickey, Sashi Perera and Bjorn Stewart all chasing the ultimate honour: exhibition glory.

synopsis.generic: Charlie Pickering is ready for 2026 as The Weekly returns with TV's best wrap of the week's news. We watch it all, so you don't have to.
synopsis.episode: If you still want to know what's happening without having to follow the daily news, Charlie Pickering watches the week's events to bring you the best wrap-up and a few laughs. With Abbey Gelmi and Hannah Gadsby.

synopsis.generic: In the grip of a mid-life crisis, Roland meets the eternal optimist Samantha and her life-loving group of dog-parkers. As Roland begrudgingly keeps returning to the park, he realises he might need a community to belong to.
synopsis.episode: It's the day of Samantha and Steve's wedding. Roland is thrilled to have Mia home again but distracted by his growing feelings for Samantha. (Final)

synopsis.generic: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Michael Tetlow.
synopsis.episode: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Jade Barker.

synopsis.generic: The return of the intense investigative thriller about the secrets that families keep from each other and which, once shared, can tear them apart forever.
synopsis.episode: With Elaine in hospital, Anna and Jenny search her home for a phone that might link her to Grace's death. Jacob turns to Mairead for help and tells her about his problems with Paul.

synopsis.generic: The return of the intense investigative thriller about the secrets that families keep from each other and which, once shared, can tear them apart forever.
synopsis.episode: Val & Paul tell the family about their plans to turn the house into a therapy centre, both Carol & Frank express their concerns. With a woman in intensive care in hospital, the Garda approach Elaine about the dent on her car.

synopsis.generic: Your weekend begins with an all-night music video marathon. rage brings you New Releases showcasing the latest in buzzed-about and boundary-pushing music videos.
Keep the party vibe going as the sun comes up with continuous music videos, from new releases to classics and the songs we've long forgotten.
Your weekend starts here as rage counts down the hottest music videos of the week, with the latest chart toppers and brand new hit predictions.
All the news you need to start your day. Fauziah Ibrahim and Johanna Nicholson bring you the latest news, interviews, politics, culture, weather and global affairs. Plus sport with Jared Coote.
synopsis.episode: All the news you need to start your weekend. Fauziah Ibrahim and Johanna Nicholson bring you the latest news, interviews, politics, culture, weather and global affairs. Plus sport with Jared Coote.

synopsis.generic: Keep the party vibe going every Saturday morning with wall-to-wall music videos, from classic hits and long-forgotten tracks from the rage vault, to the latest new releases and special features.

Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. Guest Programmers curate a playlist of their all-time favourite clips.

Melissa Mackay presents the latest from ABC News on the weekend bringing you today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport, weather and information.
synopsis.episode: Lorna Dunkley presents the latest from ABC News on the weekend bringing you today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport, weather and information.

synopsis.generic: Married police detectives Vanessa and Cole are finally leaving their cop careers in the Australian police force behind and have just invested in their dream - a cute 13 room motel in a sunny, oceanside New Zealand town.
synopsis.episode: Married ex-cops Vinny and Cole have swapped the homicide beat in Sydney for their retirement dream, running a retro seaside motel in a coastal New Zealand town. But it seems murder and mayhem have a new place to stay.

synopsis.generic: The multi award-winning much-loved crime drama Professor T is back for a third season. The Professor is in jail and must find a way to get his old life and job back.
synopsis.episode: Following a murder at a Criminology Conference, the Professor must prove his innocence to regain his reputation.

synopsis.generic: Everyday Australians get the opportunity of a lifetime as they're invited to play a public piano at unique locations around the country.
synopsis.episode: With one concert spot remaining, our everyday pianists are playing for the shoppers in the heart of Sydney's China Town.

synopsis.generic: Everyday Australians get the opportunity of a lifetime as they're invited to play a public piano at unique locations around the country.
synopsis.episode: Our very special concert is here! Mentored by Harry Connick, Jr. and Andrea Lam, each of our fabulous five were selected because they represent the sheer power of the piano. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: We meet devout members of the Aetherius Society, an international religion that believes life on Earth is guided by advanced extra-terrestrial civilisations existing in other realms on planets in our solar system.

synopsis.generic: In 2026, Logie-award-winning documentary program Australian Story celebrates 30 years sharing compelling stories about real people that illuminate, entertain and inspire. The power of the personal. Presented by Leigh Sales.
synopsis.episode: Who cares for our children when we're gone if they can't care for themselves? As Deirdre Croft faces the end of her life, she's on a mission to ensure her son is looked after when she's gone.

synopsis.generic: A tasty sampler of stories from the land - covering food, agriculture, crops, climate, business and innovation - as we meet the fascinating people who make our rural and regional communities unique. Presented by Pip Courtney.
Kath and Kim, a dysfunctional mother-daughter duo, navigate through relationship troubles, personal issues and life while trying to stay sane.
synopsis.episode: Kath is desperate to raise the money to hire a carriage for the wedding. When all else fails she holds a lingerie party, with Kim and Sharon as models. Kel asks Brett to be his best man.

synopsis.generic: Gold Logie-winning comedian Tom Gleeson has managed to herd a new bunch of self-described experts into the hallowed ABC studios to undergo the HARDEST challenge in TV, or probably the whole world.
synopsis.episode: It takes all sorts, and Hard Quiz is a big bag of liquorice! Vying for Tom's Big Brass Mug are experts in the Real Housewives of Beverly Hills, James Bond movies, the Sydney Harbour Bridge, and Victorian women's fashion!

synopsis.generic: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Guy Stayner (Mon-Fri).
synopsis.episode: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Tasmanians. Presented by Sabra Lane.

synopsis.generic: Married police detectives Vanessa and Cole are finally leaving their cop careers in the Australian police force behind and have just invested in their dream - a cute 13 room motel in a sunny, oceanside New Zealand town.
synopsis.episode: It's peak summer and the seaside town is filled with partying teenagers. When one of them goes missing, Vinny and Cole come up against a trio of intimidating, entitled teenage girls, who are hiding a secret.

synopsis.generic: Lauren and Ned abandon their exciting city lives and return to the tropical north of Australia when they learn that they are now the guardians of Lauren's orphaned eight-year-old niece.
synopsis.episode: Lauren and Ned struggle as new parents while Lauren wonders who Taya's biological father is - and if she should contact him.

synopsis.generic: Join DI Neville Parker and his team as they solve intriguing crimes on the idyllic Saint Marie. With new and returning faces and a surprising enemy, this season is filled with twists and turns.
synopsis.episode: Commissioner Selwyn Patterson struggles in the wake of his shock shooting. As the team rally round him, will he be able to bury his demons and move on? Elsewhere, the local community is rocked by a murder at a care home.

synopsis.generic: This England, is a fictionalised drama based on Boris Johnson's tumultuous first months as Prime Minister, traces the impact on the country of the first wave of the coronavirus pandemic.
synopsis.episode: Dominic Cummings holds a press conference to address his breach of lockdown regulations whilst Carrie gives birth and the country continues to cope with Covid. (Final)

synopsis.generic: Start your Sunday morning with the show the politicians rely on to find out what's going on. David Speers presents Australia's must-watch political program, joined by a panel of the best political analysts and commentators.
synopsis.episode: David Speers and the panel discuss the week in politics including the RBA's next decision on interest rates, the federal budget, plus the Liberals and One Nation face an electoral test at the South Australian State Election.

synopsis.episode: Abbey Gelmi and the panel discuss the week in sport including the Matildas at the Women's Asian Cup, AFL and NRL news, Super Rugby, A-League and more.

synopsis.episode: At Sydney's Circular Quay, amateur pianists share moving stories through music while Guy Sebastian and Andrea Lam offer once in a lifetime performance opportunity.

synopsis.generic: Domineering granny Debbie Fendon is a woman who will stop at nothing to protect her family, but the person they most need protecting from is her.
synopsis.episode: When William Fendon is mistakenly declared dead and the payout from his life insurance is sent to wife Debbie, mischievous Debbie spies a golden opportunity - and decides to keep the money while William hides in their loft.

synopsis.generic: Frauds is a story of survival, deception and revenge. It explores the complex and darkly comic relationship between our two central characters as they set out to pull off a huge art theft.
synopsis.episode: When Bert is released from prison there's only one person she can turn to; Sam. Bert rekindles their friendship but can she persuade Sam to help with her dying wish - one last job?

synopsis.generic: Married police detectives Vanessa and Cole are finally leaving their cop careers in the Australian police force behind and have just invested in their dream - a cute 13 room motel in a sunny, oceanside New Zealand town.
synopsis.episode: It's peak summer and the seaside town is filled with partying teenagers. When one of them goes missing, Vinny and Cole come up against a trio of intimidating, entitled teenage girls, who are hiding a secret.

synopsis.generic: Lauren and Ned abandon their exciting city lives and return to the tropical north of Australia when they learn that they are now the guardians of Lauren's orphaned eight-year-old niece.
synopsis.episode: Lauren and Ned struggle as new parents while Lauren wonders who Taya's biological father is - and if she should contact him.
In the anarchic town of Seaside, nowhere near the sea, puppeteers Judy and Punch are trying to resurrect their marionette show.

description.censor-warning: Adult Themes, Coarse Language, Drug Use, Horror, Nudity, Sex Scenes, Sexual References, Violence

synopsis.generic: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. We curate the best clips of all time to surprise and entertain you for hours.
Australia's Most Popular Lifestyle series returns for another season of gardening know-how and inspiration. So, let's get stuck in with Logie winning host Costa Georgiadis!
synopsis.episode: Sophie Thomson asks an expert rose pruner to share his techniques, Costa Georgiadis creates a worm hotel from a bathtub, Josh Byrne builds an edible water feature, and Jerry Coleby-Williams learns the A-Z of passionfruit.

synopsis.generic: Host Michael McCoy, one of Australia's leading landscape designers, charts the evolution of eight gardens from design plan through construction to the spectacular end results.
synopsis.episode: A couple ask their son and daughter-in-law to create a unique garden in Kyneton in Victoria. But do they have the courage to embrace a design that will transform their bland backyard into a striking sculptural showcase?

description.series-title: A Bite To Eat With Alice
description.epg-title1: A Bite To Eat With Alice

synopsis.generic: A Bite to Eat with Alice is a cooking show dedicated to creating delicious food. No matter your skill level, host Alice and her guest - who may not be able to cook toast - will inspire you to experiment in the kitchen.
synopsis.episode: Alice makes a dish inspired by her mother for the much-loved cravat-wearing cook... Matt Preston! Matt shows Alice the trick to the perfect braise, and we discover that Matt is a romantic at heart!

synopsis.generic: Start your Sunday morning with the show the politicians rely on to find out what's going on. David Speers presents Australia's must-watch political program, joined by a panel of the best political analysts and commentators.
synopsis.episode: David Speers and the panel discuss the week in politics including the RBA's next decision on interest rates, the federal budget, plus the Liberals and One Nation face an electoral test at the South Australian State Election.

synopsis.generic: As each day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato present the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy, Nate Byrne and Lillian Rangiah.
synopsis.episode: As the day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, interviews and the day's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather.

synopsis.generic: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.
synopsis.episode: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

synopsis.generic: Married police detectives Vanessa and Cole are finally leaving their cop careers in the Australian police force behind and have just invested in their dream - a cute 13 room motel in a sunny, oceanside New Zealand town.
synopsis.episode: It's peak summer and the seaside town is filled with partying teenagers. When one of them goes missing, Vinny and Cole come up against a trio of intimidating, entitled teenage girls, who are hiding a secret.

synopsis.generic: Lauren and Ned abandon their exciting city lives and return to the tropical north of Australia when they learn that they are now the guardians of Lauren's orphaned eight-year-old niece.
synopsis.episode: Lauren and Ned struggle as new parents while Lauren wonders who Taya's biological father is - and if she should contact him.

description.censor-warning: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander viewers are advised that the following program contains images and voices of people who have died

synopsis.generic: Take a road less travelled and visit some of the small towns and communities that make Australia special. Heather Ewart and guest presenters return to discover more remarkable stories and inspiring people you'll never forget.
synopsis.episode: Back Roads returns with a journey on the Strzelecki Track, starting near Lyndhurst in SA and heading into the Strzelecki Desert. Heather Ewart witnesses a way of life on this historic track that's been unchanged for decades.

synopsis.generic: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.
synopsis.episode: The leading news at lunchtime. Kathryn Robinson brings you today's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

synopsis.generic: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.

description.episode-title: Kav Temperley, Alex Lee, Odette & Dave O'Neil

synopsis.generic: Join Adam Hills, Myf Warhurst and Alan Brough for a brand new series of your favourite music quiz show. There'll be new games, fantastic live music and, as always, you can play along with all your old favourites.
synopsis.episode: You'll want to be tuning in to see this episode with Dave O'Neil, Alex Lee, Odette and Kav Temperley, as they disappoint John Travolta, try to get into a nightclub and attempt to solve this week's secret song.

synopsis.generic: Anh Do paints some of Australia's most famous faces and captures their personalities on the canvas. The charismatic comedian, writer and Archibald Finalist delves into the inspirational stories that have shaped their lives.
synopsis.episode: From the age of five, Michael Clarke dreamt of playing for Australia. Hear the personal stories behind the headlines that made him a poster boy of modern cricket. How will Anh capture his determination and schoolboy charm?

synopsis.generic: Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue and deception.
synopsis.episode: The owner of two sketchbooks found in a shed in France ask Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould to help prove they are the work of a young Toulouse Lautrec.

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: When his estranged father's drunken body is found passed out near a murder scene, Murdoch finds himself questioning his own objectivity.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two women looking for answers to questions that have haunted them for decades: a woman who made a discovery as a teenager that changed her life and a mother longing to find the son she felt she let down.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team visit more of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, inviting the public to bring their antiques for examination and share stories of how they came to own them.
synopsis.episode: A special edition of the Antiques Roadshow celebrating items with a royal connection.

synopsis.generic: Gold Logie-winning comedian Tom Gleeson has managed to herd a new bunch of self-described experts into the hallowed ABC studios to undergo the HARDEST challenge in TV, or probably the whole world.
synopsis.episode: Which subject will win Tom Gleeson's Big Brass Mug for its expert contestant on this Hard Quiz: women in Greek Mythology, pop queen Shania Twain, the 7 Up documentary series, or the mouth-watering world of spices?

synopsis.generic: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Guy Stayner (Mon-Fri).
synopsis.episode: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Tasmanians. Presented by Lucy Shannon.

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

In 2026, Logie-award-winning documentary program Australian Story celebrates 30 years sharing compelling stories about real people that illuminate, entertain and inspire. The power of the personal. Presented by Leigh Sales.

The multi-award winning Four Corners returns for another year of powerhouse investigative journalism - fearless and forensic, exposing scandals, triggering inquiries, leading debate and holding power to account for 65 years.

Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

As Trump steamrolls through the second year of his second term, Chas and John are here to guide you through the mayhem: covering Iran, ICE, tariffs, NATO, Russia/Ukraine, Gaza, affordability, Greenland, the midterms and more.
synopsis.episode: Trump's presidency steamrolls into its second year and a deeply divided America celebrates its 250th birthday. US voters will go to the polls for the midterm elections - will Republicans hold on to their majority in Congress?

synopsis.generic: We are your passport to the world. Our stories, told through the ABC's network of foreign correspondents, will take you to incredible places where you'll meet the people and see the forces shaping our world.
synopsis.episode: A revolution is taking place in Indonesia. President Prabowo Subianto is nationalising and militarising his nation's economy to deliver a self-reliant future. But some are warning Indonesia could be returning to the Suharto era.

synopsis.generic: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Michael Tetlow.

Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.
synopsis.episode: The RBA will tomorrow deliver their second interest rates decision for 2026. After hiking rates in February, will we see another rate rise or will the cash rate remain on hold? Kirsten Aiken brings you the latest.

synopsis.generic: Charlie Pickering is ready for 2026 as The Weekly returns with TV's best wrap of the week's news. We watch it all, so you don't have to.
synopsis.episode: If you still want to know what's happening without having to follow the daily news, Charlie Pickering watches the week's events to bring you the best wrap-up and a few laughs. With Abbey Gelmi and Hannah Gadsby.

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: When his estranged father's drunken body is found passed out near a murder scene, Murdoch finds himself questioning his own objectivity.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two women looking for answers to questions that have haunted them for decades: a woman who made a discovery as a teenager that changed her life and a mother longing to find the son she felt she let down.

synopsis.generic: Australia's longest running music TV show, rage is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. We curate the best clips of all time to surprise and entertain you for hours.
Australia's Most Popular Lifestyle series returns for another season of gardening know-how and inspiration. So, let's get stuck in with Logie winning host Costa Georgiadis!
synopsis.episode: Josh Byrne gives his tomatoes a head start, Tino Carnevale discovers some living plant fossils, Costa Georgiadis visits a super-productive small garden, and Jane Edmanson explores an amazing succulent garden.

description.series-title: A Bite To Eat With Alice
description.epg-title1: A Bite To Eat With Alice

synopsis.generic: A Bite to Eat with Alice is a cooking show dedicated to creating delicious food. No matter your skill level, host Alice and her guest - who may not be able to cook toast - will inspire you to experiment in the kitchen.
synopsis.episode: Author and wild woman Gina Chick stops by and together she and Alice tackle a whole snapper. Alice forages ingredients from her fridge cooking her Fridge Forage Minestrone for Gina.

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

As each day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato present the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy, Nate Byrne and Lillian Rangiah.
synopsis.episode: As the day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, interviews and the day's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather.

synopsis.generic: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.
synopsis.episode: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

synopsis.generic: In 2026, Logie-award-winning documentary program Australian Story celebrates 30 years sharing compelling stories about real people that illuminate, entertain and inspire. The power of the personal. Presented by Leigh Sales.

As Trump steamrolls through the second year of his second term, Chas and John are here to guide you through the mayhem: covering Iran, ICE, tariffs, NATO, Russia/Ukraine, Gaza, affordability, Greenland, the midterms and more.
synopsis.episode: Trump's presidency steamrolls into its second year and a deeply divided America celebrates its 250th birthday. US voters will go to the polls for the midterm elections - will Republicans hold on to their majority in Congress?

synopsis.generic: Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: We meet devout members of the Aetherius Society, an international religion that believes life on Earth is guided by advanced extra-terrestrial civilisations existing in other realms on planets in our solar system.

synopsis.generic: 'Unprecedented' is a word we hear a lot lately. But the topics in the news are rarely unprecedented, often we've seen it all before. What can we learn from history? Matt Bevan digs into the past to understand current events.
synopsis.episode: In 1959 travel was getting faster and many expected we'd be routinely zipping to other planets in no time. But now, our transport is not much faster than it was back then, so why are we still so slow? (Part 3 of 4)

synopsis.generic: The leading news at lunchtime. Ros Childs brings you the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter to you.
Join DI Neville Parker and his team as they solve intriguing crimes on the idyllic Saint Marie. With new and returning faces and a surprising enemy, this season is filled with twists and turns.
synopsis.episode: Commissioner Selwyn Patterson struggles in the wake of his shock shooting. As the team rally round him, will he be able to bury his demons and move on? Elsewhere, the local community is rocked by a murder at a care home.

description.episode-title: Andrea Keller, Reuben Kaye, Briggs & Zoe Coombs-Marr

synopsis.generic: Join Adam Hills, Myf Warhurst and Alan Brough for a brand new series of your favourite music quiz show. There'll be new games, fantastic live music and, as always, you can play along with all your old favourites.
synopsis.episode: Joining Alan and Myf are Briggs, Andrea Keller, Reuben Kaye and Zoe Coombs-Marr. Have they got the brains to work out the secret song? Features violinist Eric Avery plus a new cute game: The Kids are Alright.

synopsis.generic: Anh Do paints some of Australia's most famous faces and captures their personalities on the canvas. The charismatic comedian, writer and Archibald Finalist delves into the inspirational stories that have shaped their lives.
synopsis.episode: Anh is joined by Lindy Chamberlain. Her wrongful conviction for the death of her daughter Azaria in 1980, taken by a dingo from their campsite, is etched into our national psyche. Can Anh capture her strength and humanity?

synopsis.generic: Philip Mould and Fiona Bruce investigate more thrilling cases of art world mystery, intrigue and deception.
synopsis.episode: In this episode, the team investigates whether a small watercolour sketch could be the work of the great British 20th century sculptor Henry Moore.

synopsis.generic: Set in 1895 Toronto, Murdoch Mysteries explores the intriguing world of William Murdoch, a handsome young detective using radical forensic techniques to solve some of the city's most gruesome murders.
synopsis.episode: When a skeletal corpse falls to the stage during a performance, Murdoch discovers not only a sinister murder plot, but Brackenreid's secret love for the theatre.

synopsis.generic: Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, the series helps a handful of people, some of whom have been searching in vain for many years, find the family members they are desperately seeking.
synopsis.episode: Two stories about a man and a woman, each looking for their birth mother. Our second story is on behalf of a man longing to find his birth mother after more than fifty years apart.

synopsis.generic: Fiona Bruce and the Roadshow team visit more of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, inviting the public to bring their antiques for examination and share stories of how they came to own them.
synopsis.episode: The roadshow comes from the sunny surroundings of Swanage Pier in Dorset.

synopsis.generic: Gold Logie-winning comedian Tom Gleeson has managed to herd a new bunch of self-described experts into the hallowed ABC studios to undergo the HARDEST challenge in TV, or probably the whole world.
synopsis.episode: Tom Gleeson and his Big Brass Mug are besieged by another four contestants with eclectic expert subjects: Cronulla Sharks fan Matt, Mulan lover Jessica, Doja Cat fan Elle, and Brad, who's really into vintage roller skates.

synopsis.generic: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Guy Stayner (Mon-Fri).
synopsis.episode: News from where we live. ABC News Tasmania brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to Tasmanians. Presented by Guy Stayner.

synopsis.generic: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

We are your passport to the world. Our stories, told through the ABC's network of foreign correspondents, will take you to incredible places where you'll meet the people and see the forces shaping our world.

Todd Sampson explores the intriguing complexities of human behaviour, driven by a curiosity to unravel the mysteries behind our actions. Each episode embarks on a quest to understand the things people do.
synopsis.episode: How did risking your life become a hobby? Combine a healthy dose of social media, the addictive rush of adrenaline, and the rise in extreme sports, like free climbing and BASE jumping, and you might get close to an answer.

synopsis.generic: 'Unprecedented' is a word we hear a lot lately. But the topics in the news are rarely unprecedented, often we've seen it all before. What can we learn from history? Matt Bevan digs into the past to understand current events.
synopsis.episode: During the Cold War, it was a very real fear that a nuclear winter would obliterate life as we know it. In 2026 the sense of dread has been replaced by the threat of climate change. So are we destined to feel doomed? (Part 4)

synopsis.generic: The six-part arts series with Virginia Trioli is back for a third season, exploring the essence of imagination and creativity with some of Australia's best creative minds.
synopsis.episode: The actor's actor, Hugo Weaving, finds the humanity within the fractured and complex men he plays in movies and films, but is still a beloved icon of the Australian stage.

synopsis.generic: Compass explores the Australian soul - our beliefs, our ethical dilemmas and the changing face of our spirituality, with host, popular journalist and broadcaster, Indira Naidoo.
synopsis.episode: Writer Benjamin Law explores the increasing number of people turning to AI to help them deal with personal and existential crises. He discovers that while it can help some, it can also have devastating consequences.

synopsis.generic: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Michael Tetlow.

Australia's leading daily business and finance show, bringing you up to date on market moves, economic trends and the forces affecting consumers, employees and business owners big and small. Presented by Kirsten Aiken.
synopsis.episode: The RBA have delivered their second interest rates decision for 2026. After hiking rates in February, will we see another rise? Kirsten Aiken brings you reaction and analysis from markets, economists and borrowers.

Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.
